item.title: An answer to a question I’ve always pondered
item.content:encoded: Now, after all these years, my question of why Gold medal Olympian’s tend to fade into obscurity is answered. Upvote ;)

item.title: "Nuke it" made for a lackluster ending
item.content:encoded:
Really enjoyed it! Interesting premise and all of the descriptions and logs were great, the ever-increasing mystery and dread was really getting to me.

The end plan for Thriambus lacked punch though: I was expecting the Foundation to do something more precise than "hitting Omega with literally 40 nukes." It feels sort of cartoonish in comparison to the nuanced use of information throughout.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Independent of the Foundation, Baron Pierre de Coubertin was attempting to create a modern equivalent to the ancient Olympic Games.

Glad to see this. It's all personal preference of course but I'm not fond of the "significant historical event only happened because of an SCP/the Foundation" preferring the idea of real history happening alongside the anomalous. So this bit removed the only part of the article putting me off!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, someone vandalized it. nm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was this entry deleted?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A minor point: is it worth censoring the name of the first president of the IOC if I can just look him up on Wikipedia?

Otherwise, I quite enjoyed "the Olympics are the qualifying round for a real-life MOBA against Hades with escalating difficulty and apocalyptic stakes".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of this and the ties to greek mythology are fun, but there are some issues with it that have pushed me from upvote to novote. First of all, you've got a lot of redactions and blackboxing here. Far too much, and a lot of it reads to me as "thing I didn't want to write" rather than "information that needs to be removed". That the whole mention of HMCL supervisor irks me too, as a general case. It's a nonsense term that means nothing and even if you buy the retroactively made-up meaning for it (I don't, personally), it doesn't seem appropriate here.

The last addendum though, feels almost farcical to me. The over-the-top "all the nukes!!!" play here is very hard to take seriously, especially because as far as I can tell it's based on nothing but speculation. They are just guessing that Omega will cross over into the real world if enough games are failed, and they apparently know close to nothing about this entity in the first place, certainly not enough to warrant such a reaction. Not to mention that should that whole sequence play out, that's basically the same as a broken veil scenario; there's no way the public would buy any of those restrictions and then believe terrorists somehow set off 20 nukes and launched all the worlds ICMBs at it.

Other issues
– hide block

Some other minor issues.

and upwards towards [REDACTED] days SCP-4000-1 time, though longer periods of time have been observed.

This sentence reads very badly; the "upwards towards [redacted]" seems like its missing a word or has an extra word, but based on the wording of it, it implies the upper limit of the time for this thing, so how could longer periods then be observed?

SCP-4000-1 is an Earth-like planet orbiting a star in the Alpha Herculis system.

Tying this to an actual star system feels unnecessary to me. It adds nothing and the idea that this somehow physically exists out there in space actually takes away from the whole idea a little.

Description: First ELEIA event with no survivors. Specifics of the event remain unknown at this time. SCP-████ considered lost.

Why would an SCP object be lost here? Why would they have an SCP with them at all?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
can’t wait to read all of the hopes and dream of writers scps and crush them, starting with you

Ouch, I accidentally cut myself on that edge!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, if I'm getting this right, there was about 1,400 years or so without olympic games, and during that time there would have been over 350 Eleia events, moving the torch at least 14,000 km closer to the temple of the potentially XK monster - a far longer distance than a 140,000 km2 landmass could ever encompass.

But that doesn't seem feasible, given the monster actively resists and gets more violent every time the athletes are successful. Not to mention the implication being ancient greeks somewhat succeeded or fared rather well, at least, where people with modern technology are getting mauled, to the lack of a better word.

It would mean, in theory, contrary to what it is stated, that nothing happens as long as there is no Olympic Games, and the Foundation is spending a huge ammount of resources and streching suspension of disbelief with all those athletes dying when it could cancel the games - a small price to normality given the risk of an apocalypse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept, and it's well written.

One quibble though.
The line below to me would sound less awkward as:
"Likewise, an arbitrary amount number of Olympic events is are to be added each year to ensure a successful ELEIA event1 event."

Otherwise, well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
wtf is a indigenous fauna? i though this is a scp article, the reader is suppose to be understand the info you want them to know, not stuck in confusion wondering what a indigenous fauna

"Fauna" is a pretty common word. It basically just means animals ("flora and fauna" is a phrase used colloquially, not just clinically. It means "plants and animals").

"Indigenous" is just "stuff that naturally lives there", instead of invasive species or plants/animals humans brought to a place that didn't have any before. This is a word that's often used by nature documentaries or other nature-focused media.

or some kind of fancy long word you hamfisted in.

Neither of the words used above are hamfisted. Reader familiarity with certain terms may vary, but when writing an SCP article, it's better to err on the more professional side than the more casual side. Worst comes worst, a reader can look up the definition of the words they're not familiar with and learn for future readings. Better to encourage a familiarity with professional wording than to just expect to drag down the vocabulary range. Set the bar high, etc.

item.title: Re: Moderator Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: fine, then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Its a rather silly article, but I'll fully admit it had me entertained from start to finish.

The idea of the GOC and SCPF rigging the Olympics so as to launch an extradimensional assault is quite entertaining, and the various logs of the buildup of this event over the years were engaging.

The article itself is not without its plot holes, however. As pointed above, the idea of the Foundation so easily covering up famous athlete deaths is rather laughable (I mean, good luck on that if Michael Phelps got axed), and the fact that the GOC/SCPF are having such a hard time with modern weaponry and tactics while the Greeks were good to go with hoplites and spears is a bit of an immersion breaker.

Still, the article is fun, and for that, I feel like a +1 is not out of line.

item.title: Re: Moderator Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Even in its unfinished state, your comment was extremely unhelpful. Your attitude was sub-par; your formatting made it difficult to differentiate your critique from the parts of the article; and as evidenced by your unawareness as to the role of amnestics, your existing critique failed to accurately assess the article and elements that needed revision. I strongly suggest that you re-read the criticism policy (and Metacritique One) before finishing your comment.

item.title: Re: Moderator Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: can you discuss this after i finish my comment

item.title: Moderator Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: louis0908, this is both unhelpful, overly vehement and attacks the author instead of criticising the work, violating the http://www.scp-wiki.net/criticism-policy. Please don't do this again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
as you can see, i include every parts of the description, which if you dont know what that mean, EVERY PARTS OF IT IS FUCKED.

Nope, can't tell at all, because you barely touched on anything worth criticizing.

can’t wait to read all of the hopes and dream of writers scps and crush them, starting with you, this is going to be a 5 part analysis in the following order

Nah fam, your incorrect grammars ain't gonna make it fly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
how the fuck did the foundation safely fly a bunch of fucking plane then drop amnestics without missing a single person remember the damn athele but ALSO didn’t remember the damn plane.

Mate it's a plane chock-full with amnestics. If you don't know it's a term for a drug that could erase memories. Why wouldn't it erase the memories of it itself being administered? And we live in modern age where fighter planes could fly above a city for acrobatics with colorful smoke. People could easily dismiss it as part of the Olympics.

Your attitude isn't helping yourself here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
its the scp-4000 contest, its that time of the year again! can’t wait to read all of the hopes and dream of writers scps and crush them, starting with you, this is going to be a 5 part analysis in the following order

+ Show Block
- Close

1. special containment procedures
2. description
3. addendum
4. how to make it better
5. overall

im only going to mention notable parts of the scp. so let get started.

1. special containment procedure

*In instances where a lost athlete's identity is too deeply ingrained in popular culture or public knowledge, wide scale Class A amnestic compounds are to be employed to all major population centers via airborne distribution if no other options of erasure are viable.*

WHAT! first of all, this is stupid, second, how the fuck did the foundation safely fly a bunch of fucking plane then drop amnestics without missing a single person remember the damn athele but ALSO didn’t remember the damn plane, what if a guy is in his bedroom sleeping with all of his window closed? i know that this only happen if no option of erasure are viable, but what are thoose option? third, this implies the scp must be very important to the foundation, dropping amniestic in a heavily populated area is the shit you see everyday, too bad its held back by the stupidity plagued this scp i will shown later on. well this is a detailed and logical start, i can’t wait for the description.

2. description

*SCP-4000 is a series of phenomena which occur 1-24 hours following the extinguishing of the Olympic flame in the closing ceremony of the Summer Olympic Games3. During SCP-4000, all athletes winning a gold medal in that year's Olympic Games undergo temporal and spatial displacement, seemingly being removed from standard reality. While displaced, the athletes will manifest on SCP-4000-1. Displacement lasts for one picosecond local time and upwards towards [REDACTED] days SCP-4000-1 time, though longer periods of time have been observed.

SCP-4000-1 is an Earth-like planet orbiting a star in the Alpha Herculis system. The surface of SCP-4000-1 is comprised mainly of ocean, with the only land being a small continent with an estimated size of 140,000 square kilometers4. During an ELEIA event, competitors are faced with opposition from the planet's SCP-4000 is a series of phenomena which occur 1-24 hours following the extinguishing of the Olympic flame in the closing ceremony of the Summer Olympic Games3. During SCP-4000, all athletes winning a gold medal in that year's Olympic Games undergo temporal and spatial displacement, seemingly being removed from standard reality. While displaced, the athletes will manifest on SCP-4000-1. Displacement lasts for one picosecond local time and upwards towards [REDACTED] days SCP-4000-1 time, though longer periods of time have been observed.

SCP-4000-1 is an Earth-like planet orbiting a star in the Alpha Herculis system. The surface of SCP-4000-1 is comprised mainly of ocean, with the only land being a small continent with an estimated size of 140,000 square kilometers4. During an ELEIA event, competitors are faced with opposition from the planet's indigenous fauna, collectively designated SCP-4000-α. SCP-4000-α will actively attempt to prevent the completion of the event, displaying a level of strategy that implies high intelligence or some sort of collective shared mind. While acquiring a sample of SCP-4000-α, living or otherwise, is currently impossible, some hypotheses can be formed from visual records. SCP-4000-α often resemble species found on Earth, though with drastic differences in biology. Instances often will have multiple heads or traits from several different species. Due to SCP-4000-1 having similar planetary conditions to those found on Earth, it is unknown how SCP-4000-α naturally evolved to this state, though cross-contamination from a previous ELEIA event is one possibility. The currently accepted hypothesis assumes that SCP-4000-Ω may be responsible for the creation of SCP-4000-α5. Structures found on SCP-4000-1 bear visual resemblance to those found in ancient Greece, though the exact style is unlike those found on Earth. One such structure, a large temple, is where MTF Omega-14 will manifest. The temple is inhabited by SCP-4000-2.

SCP-4000-2 is a humanoid entity, resembling an adult male of Mediterranean descent. SCP-4000-2 has displayed several traits similar to those of reality benders, namely the displacement of MTF Omega-14 members and the ability to insert information into an observer's mind6, though it is unknown if these are the extents of the entity's abilities. Upon manifestation of MTF Omega-14, they will be greeted by SCP-4000-2, who will explain and then instigate an ELEIA event. Upon completion of an ELEIA event, all living members of MTF Omega-14 will return to their positions prior to displacement., collectively designated SCP-4000-α. SCP-4000-α will actively attempt to prevent the completion of the event, displaying a level of strategy that implies high intelligence or some sort of collective shared mind. While acquiring a sample of SCP-4000-α, living or otherwise, is currently impossible, some hypotheses can be formed from visual records. SCP-4000-α often resemble species found on Earth, though with drastic differences in biology. Instances often will have multiple heads or traits from several different species. Due to SCP-4000-1 having similar planetary conditions to those found on Earth, it is unknown how SCP-4000-α naturally evolved to this state, though cross-contamination from a previous ELEIA event is one possibility. The currently accepted hypothesis assumes that SCP-4000-Ω may be responsible for the creation of SCP-4000-α5. Structures found on SCP-4000-1 bear visual resemblance to those found in ancient Greece, though the exact style is unlike those found on Earth. One such structure, a large temple, is where MTF Omega-14 will manifest. The temple is inhabited by SCP-4000-2.

SCP-4000-2 is a humanoid entity, resembling an adult male of Mediterranean descent. SCP-4000-2 has displayed several traits similar to those of reality benders, namely the displacement of MTF Omega-14 members and the ability to insert information into an observer's mind6, though it is unknown if these are the extents of the entity's abilities. Upon manifestation of MTF Omega-14, they will be greeted by SCP-4000-2, who will explain and then instigate an ELEIA event. Upon completion of an ELEIA event, all living members of MTF Omega-14 will return to their positions prior to displacement.*

as you can see, i include every parts of the description, which if you dont know what that mean, EVERY PARTS OF IT IS FUCKED.
first of all, what will trigger an ELEIA event? if a rich cunt decided to make a mini olympic event for him and his friend, and he win the gold medal, set the large torch on fire and extinguise it, after 1-24 hours, will he be teleported to scp-4000-1? nevermind that, let just ignore that and move on, we still have plenty.
wtf is a indigenous fauna? i though this is a scp article, the reader is suppose to be understand the info you want them to know, not stuck in confusion wondering what a indigenous fauna or some kind of fancy long word you hamfisted in.

(to be continued)

Please collapse long posts ~ Leveritas

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, so this is basically one of those 'The Foundation actually did X' but for the Olympics.

One of my favourite entries in the contest so far. I like the entire idea of it. (even if it was used before)

Thats a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the current one a clone or Foundation or GOC operatives?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your presumption about the medal is correct, only the person who got the gold medal during the olympics would be effected.

I doubt an ELEIA event would care if an athlete was using steroids if stuff like Orange Suits are allowed. Anti-doping rules would be put in place to ensure that no civilians are able to outperform Foundation/GOC plants and also to make the Olympics/IOC seem reputable. It also provides a great cover story if a well known athlete died during 4000, just tell the public they were caught with steroids.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quite like this.

One question that did occur - what happens when a Gold medal is retrospectively awarded due to the original winner being disqualified due to drugs?

I presume the original winner is displaced during the event, and there's no temporal element that retrospectively inserts the new winner.

Just wondered whether the rules of the event would penalise drug use. Which is one of the reasons for the strict anti-doping rules and sanctions in Olympic Sports, to minimise negative consequences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addressed, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4000-1 is an Earth-like planet orbiting the star Alpha Herculis.

Alpha Herculis is not a star. It is a triple star system.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kept going back and forth over the balance of silliness of the base concept versus level of fun and exactly how much you were expecting me to suspend my disbelief/take seriously. The balance was evening out towards the end, but the super-stark super-serious last addendum shifted this from a +1 to a no-vote.

Apparently I'm willing to accept "the Foundation rigs the Olympics and world-famous athletes' deaths are easily covered up" in exchange for "SCP/GOC extradimensional fun run!", but "eldritch XK 500 nukes in the Parthenon" is a bridge too far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am in love with this entry! I felt a little confused about the Greek culture parallels, is it implying that Greek culture was actually based on this alien god entity? Also, how about the time before the Olympics ever began? Did they accidentally stumble into SCP-4000 during the first olympics and go “I guess we’re gonna right this thing now”?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’d reccomend having some sort of clarification in the article itself that regarding the time gap between the original and revived Olympics, maybe just something like a reference in a recovered Greek text about the fire’s previous location.

Other than that, solid read, and nice concept!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you, I'm glad you liked the article!

As for the GOC's involvement, destroying anomalies is their thing. While the Foundation does have quite a powerful arsenal at their disposal, they lack the highly specific combat oriented equipment that the GOC uses. Something like an Orange Suit is far beyond most of the Foundation's combat tech, and would be invaluable when the top priority is "Kill as many monsters as you can". The partnership also occurred far before Omega was considered a serious threat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, +1. But, why would the Foundation ever need the GOC's help with this? We are sacrificing anywhere between 2 to 9 anomalous objects every four years in order to keep this contained. Granted, this is a pretty big deal… but it seems like we've already accepted that 4000-omega is going to escape at some point. Plus, it states in the article that in the 1890s the Foundation high command literally voted to just reinstate the olympic games. That's kind of a big deal and implies that the Foundation has a decent amount of power in the world… just doesn't make sense that we'd need the GOC's help considering the amount of power we have. Other than that gripe, I really like this. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ELEIA-2016

Agent C████ M██████, the only survivor of the event,

You killed Michael Phelps. You bastard.

(And Andy Murray, Rafael Nadal, Usain Bolt, Kevin Durant, the entire Brazilian football team, the entire US women gymnastics team… wow, they must lose a TON of young gymnastic champions every time…)

But you also killed Ryan Lochte, so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a coincidence

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of Wu Tang Clan Ain't Nothin To Fuck To, were you inspired by that or was it a coincidence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. I had a few thoughts during the article, at least a few of which were addressed directly, and the rest were forgotten, so they must not have been important. :B Upvote after the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved It, and i couldn't avoid to love It being both an history teacher (… Yeah, unironically) and a lover of myths.

Just don't tell me that I should think about Hades being Evil. That dude is Pretty funny in the myths, a real good guy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe the idea wasn't It but after all Greece had a culture encouraging young men to follow the steps of Heroes and become warriors and masters of sports.

Maybe some priest Just knew very well what to write in those myths

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really great. Favorite "foundation did it" explanation of historical/current events I've read so far.

Only thing I think is missing is more crumbs about the nature of the alpha entities and their combat capabilities. "Twisted earth critters" isn't much to go on when it's implied that these things can bring down Orange suits

Either just some small crumbs sprinkled in other sections or an ezra addendum detailing "alpha instances" would be a good opportunity for some nasty-describing

IE: 4000-Alpha-1, 4000-Alpha-2, etc. Having some redacted to imply there's more in there

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, I'm glad you liked it!

As the Foundation and GOC throw better and stronger soldiers at 4000, the silhouette also steps up its game so that it will always be slightly ahead. It only gets directly involved once stuff like Orange Suits get used. There's also 1400 years of exponentially multiplying mythological creatures waiting for them, way more than the ancient Greeks had to go up against. The Foundation predecessor also could have had some crazy streamlined way of dealing with 4000, its just been lost to time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's Gantz as an SCP and I love it.

The reason its so close is because there were no Olympics for around 1400 years.

I find it hard to believe that the Greeks were able to effectively hold this thing off but highly trained, superbly equipped Foundation/GOC agents aren't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keep it in. If Res never continues, then it won't matter. If it does… planned events include a plot twist that could result in the construction of more suits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a good point about the suit. I mostly included it to show what kind of force the Foundation is throwing at 4000 since the suit is shown to be fairly capable in combat, especially in Number One with a Bullet. The suits used by Omega-14 would be weaker versions likely derived from less anomalous tech than Adams' suit, though still capable in combat. I can remove the reference if you'd like, since it could potentially mess with Resurrection's plot line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only thing I don't like about this is that in canon, the Number One With A Bullet suit is unique to the Resurrection canon, linked specifically to Agent Adams, and can't be reproduced for reasons that we didn't quite get to during Res but may get around to in the future.

On the other hand, there is no canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aha. My line of thinking had been 'they didn't have the Olympics for 1400 years, and nothing bad happened then'.

Thanks for clearing that up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the 'hypotheses' catch, I knew it looked wrong somehow.

Page capitalization may be an error on my part. I was always taught to capitalize the nouns and verbs and to leave the smaller words in lowercase, so that's what I did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Semi-related, in the first quote from up above, where it says "some hypothesizes can be formed", that should be "some hypotheses can be formed". Hypothesizes is a verb, and that's grammatically incorrect.

Also, is there any reason the page title isn't uniformly capitalized?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, I'm glad you liked it!

If the Olympics don't occur then it's an automatically failed ELEIA event, meaning the Silhouette gets one step closer to Earth. The reason its so close is because there were no Olympics for around 1400 years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My favourite contest entry so far!

But I can't seem to find any reason The Foundation didn't just stop holding the Olympics?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Altered a line to address that, thanks once again!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow you're fast.

Just another plot hole I noticed: in the description you said this about the Alpha:

While acquiring a sample of SCP-4000-α, living or otherwise, is currently impossible, some hypothesizes can be formed from visual records

But in Addendum C you wrote:

At the point where MTF Omega-14 would normally re-manifest, an instance of SCP-4000-α appeared instead

Upon her death, several instances of SCP-4000-α manifested in the location Agent M██████ had re-manifested

If I'm right in thinking that the contestants "re-manifest" on Earth, then the Alpha could have been captured and studied.

Other than that, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The important thing in life is not the triumph but the struggle, the essential thing is not to have conquered but to have fought well." -Pierre de Coubertin

Here it is! My entry for the 4000 contest! A huge thanks to Countless Skies, DrFred, and Leveritas for their feedback and critique, with an even more huge thanks to HumidHermit who gave feedback and critique on this in both Ideas and Brainstorming and Drafts and Critiques! They were all a tremendous help and I wouldn't have been able to write this without them.

+ Image Attributions
- Image Attributions

In order of appearance:

'Temple.png' is from Wikimedia and was edited by me.

'Document.jpg' is from Wikimedia and was not edited.

'Silhouette.png' is comprised of three different images (1, by VasCr on Wikimedia, 2, from Wikimedia, and 3, by Grizzli on Wikimedia) and was edited by me.
